items.title: Vancouver council votes not to sanction mayor after investigation found he harassed councillor
items.summary_auto: All six of Ken Sim's ABC party members voted on Tuesday against acting on the findings of the Integrity Commissioner's investigation into Sim's actions against Councillor Sean Orr. The other four councillors, including Orr, voted for sanctioning Sim,

items.title: Smith says she asked Carney to 'work with me' to lower Alberta's separatist sentiment
items.summary_auto: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith said she told Prime Minister Mark Carney 'we have a very serious problem' with her province's separatist movement during Carney's early days in office. 'There were legitimate issues in Alberta that caused people to lose hope in Canada,' Smith said. 'The prime minister and I together decided that we were going to work to address them.'

items.title: First Nation leaders speak about Pelican Narrows shooting, violence in northern Sask.
items.summary_auto: Pelican Narrows declared a state of emergency in 2024 amid ongoing violence and has since enacted a series of measures aimed at reducing crime.

items.title: N.S. to create centralized daycare waitlist to streamline search for child care
items.summary_auto: A long-awaited centralized daycare waitlist will make it easier for families to find suitable child care in Nova Scotia, officials with the Education Department said Wednesday. The province is in the early stages of building a new online portal set to launch in 2027.

items.title: Chief says fatal crash highlights 20-year fight to pave road to First Nation in central Alberta
items.summary_auto: People who regularly travel on O’Chiese Road are exposed to a higher level of risk for road injuries, according to a 2025 report commissioned by the O'Chiese First Nation.

items.title: Alberta to eliminate paper health cards, introduce integrated driver licences July 2
items.summary_auto: Alberta will start to roll out new drivers licences starting July 2 that will include an individual’s health card number, finally eliminating the paper cards that have been the bane of Albertans for decades.

items.title: Nostalgia alert: MusiquePlus is relaunching on TikTok
items.summary_auto: Long before your feed told you what to listen to, Quebec culture had a different tastemaker: MusiquePlus. Well, music fans can rejoice now that the TV channel that helped launch the careers of many Quebec stars is being revived as a new kind of influencer on TikTok.

items.title: 4 migrant farm workers in Italy burned to death in car, according to survivor, police
items.summary_auto: Four migrants working as fruit pickers in slave-like conditions in southern Italy have been burned to death. The murder case is prompting fresh national soul searching over the exploitation of farm workers.

items.title: Marine Atlantic is a ‘lifeline’ for these N.L. communities. How would a strike affect them?
items.summary_auto: Newfoundland’s Port of Argentia is a tourism hub with booming Marine Atlantic ferry bookings but a looming strike means the terminal could soon go quiet — and that has people worried.

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit St. Petersburg oil terminal before city hosts Russian economic forum
items.summary_auto: Ukrainian long-range drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and set it ablaze, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Wednesday, sending smoke billowing over the city where President Vladimir Putin was born as it hosts Russia's leading event for attracting foreign capital.

items.title: South Park creators remain defiant, promise more Trump takedowns in new season
items.summary_auto: South Park creators Matt Stone and Trey Parker say they aren’t backing down from satirizing U.S. President Trump in the show’s upcoming season — even if, as they say, the latest group trying to censor them now “has a military.”

items.title: U Sports to introduce women's flag football as a pilot sport
items.summary_auto:
Canadian university sport will introduce women's flag football as a pilot sport. U Sports and Football Canada will collaborate on flag
football's introduction and execution for the 2027-28 season.

items.title: Ottawa says CRTC's new cash requirements from streamers risk price hikes for Canadians
items.summary_auto: The federal government will direct its broadcast and telecommunications regulator to revisit a recent decision to triple the funds that streaming services like Netflix must contribute to Canadian content.

items.title: B.C. Ferries adding 5% fuel surcharge starting in mid-June
items.summary_auto: B.C. Ferries says the elevated price of fuel, driven by the U.S.-Israel war on Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, has meant it has had to absorb rising costs in recent months.

items.title: Carney says latest Trump tariffs 'not a surprise' after U.S. promises new 10% levy on Canada
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney says he isn't surprised by the Trump administration's plan to slap import levies on goods allegedly made with forced labour.

items.title: Canada’s Felix Auger-Aliassime eliminated from French Open
items.summary_auto: Felix Auger-Aliassime’s run at the French Open ended on Wednesday in Paris after dropping a 6-4, 4-6, 4-6, 4-6 quarterfinal match to Italian Flavio Cobolli.

items.title: Céline Dion pays tribute to duet partner Peabo Bryson after R&B singer's death at 75
items.summary_auto: Grammy-winning R&B singer Peabo Bryson, the voice behind Disney film duets Beauty and the Beast with Céline Dion and Aladdin's A Whole New World with Regina Belle, has died. He was 75.

items.title: Protests erupt in U.K. after video released showing arrest of dying teen
items.summary_auto: Hundreds gathered in Southampton, U.K., on Tuesday to protest over the death of 18-year-old student Henry Nowak. Police handcuffed Nowak after an incident where he was stabbed. His killer falsely accused him of a racist attack.

items.title: Public left wondering if they could be among 652 victims of Saanich, B.C., voyeur
items.summary_auto: The community of Saanich, B.C., has been told a man spent six years secretly filming hundreds of women and girls in public spaces and the private of their homes — but the vast majority of victims may still not know it happened to them.

items.title: Still no triage liaison physicians in Alberta ERs, 4 months after province's pledge
items.summary_auto: More than four months after pledging that emergency physicians would soon begin roles monitoring waiting room patients, the Alberta government’s promised triage liaison physicians aren’t yet on the job.

items.title: Are corner stores a thing of the past for Calgary — or is a renaissance around the corner?
items.summary_auto: For kids, they represent independence — the freedom of a first dollar spent. For adults, a fail-safe for the forgetful, where last-minute ingredients save a recipe. It feels like these shops are always there when needed, but the reality is that corner stores are disappearing from major cities.

items.title: Regina alternative school graduates 1st class of Grade 12 students in its 53-year history
items.summary_auto: When Asia Mills-Daniels receives her Grade 12 diploma this month she will be the first member of her family to graduate high school and a member of her high school’s first ever Grade 12 graduating class.

items.title: Zellers plays with toys, food as retailer's relaunch reaches Ontario
items.summary_auto: A new discount storefront is set to open near Toronto's Yorkdale Mall on June 18th, and another at Windsor's Tecumseh Mall in July.

items.title: IWK leans on Nova Scotia Health for radiology help amid shortage
items.summary_auto: The IWK Health Centre is leaning on colleagues at Nova Scotia Health as the Halifax hospital contends with an ongoing shortage of radiologists and works through the result of a workplace assessment in an effort to “rebuild” its diagnostic imaging department.

items.title: Iranian drone attack on Kuwait's airport kills 1, wounds dozens
items.summary_auto: Gulf hostilities flared again Wednesday as Iranian attacks on Kuwait damaged its airport and injured dozens while the U.S. military carried out strikes near the Strait of Hormuz, with ‌diplomacy to halt the war showing little sign of progress.

items.title: Sheshatshiu Innu First Nation Chief 'not giving up' on Labrador community mourning death of young woman
items.summary_auto: As a central Labrador community mourns the death of a young woman, Sheshatshiu Innu First Nation Chief Eugene Hart's message is clear: he’ll walk with the people of the town every step of the way to enact change.

items.title: AI agents lag far behind human workers. Why are tech companies laying off the humans?
items.summary_auto: AI-related layoffs are in full swing as tech companies invest in artificial intelligence agents they say will take over tasks traditionally done by humans. But a major AI infrastructure and software company says the agents fail to produce professionally acceptable work more than 19 times out of 20, and some analysts say the technology is overhyped and being using as an excuse for laying off workers.

items.title: Most of Ontario's 2024 high school grads didn't complete e-learning requirement, data shows
items.summary_auto: The vast majority of Ontario’s 2023-2024 graduating class did not complete the Ford government’s mandatory e-learning requirements, data from the province's Ministry of Education reveals.

items.title: What Canadians need to know about a 'technical recession'
items.summary_auto: While the term technical recession may have some usefulness as a political tool, does it actually have economic significance? CBC News looked into the definition of a technical recession, how it differs from a regular recession and whether Canadians need to be concerned.

items.title: Canada endorses plan to send remaining Marineland belugas to Spain, across U.S.
items.summary_auto: The Department of Fisheries and Oceans Canada has endorsed a rescue plan that would see all of Marineland's remaining beluga whales split up and sent to Oceanogràfic València in Spain or one of four U.S. locations.

items.title: Above World Cup venues, fighter jets will be on patrol
items.summary_auto: Fighter jets will be on patrol over Vancouver and Toronto over the next several weeks, protecting World Cup match venues and fan festivals.

items.title: She famously battled a gas company and won millions in settlements. Now, Erin Brockovich is taking on AI
items.summary_auto: Erin Brockovich, the renowned consumer advocate and environmental activist who inspired an Oscar-winning movie staring Julia Roberts, is now setting her sights on AI data centres with a new website that maps their growth across the U.S. and asks residents to report their concerns.

items.title: U.S. Coast Guard launches new sail drones on Lake Erie for first time
items.summary_auto: Two bright orange autonomous sail drones are floating in Lake Erie right now, part of a new U.S. Coast Guard program.

items.title: CBS News fires veteran 60 Minutes correspondent Scott Pelley after clash with new leadership
items.summary_auto: CBS News has confirmed it fired veteran 60 Minutes correspondent Scott Pelley in the wake of a publicly disclosed clash with new executive producer Nick Bilton.

items.title: First Nation in B.C. opposed to potential Alberta oil pipeline routes, says chief
items.summary_auto: West Moberly First Nations Chief Roland Willson says his community is opposed to the three oil pipeline routes being considered by the Alberta government that would run through northern B.C., with the routes recently  mapped in documents obtained  by CBC News.

items.title: 2 missing miners face ‘horrendous’ conditions, says Laos cave rescuer | Hanomansing Tonight
items.summary_auto: The search continues for two miners who remain trapped in a flooded cave in Laos. Rescuer Josh Richards talks about the tight, claustrophobic conditions they are dealing with.

items.title: Trump administration is scrapping $1.8B US fund meant to compensate president's allies
items.summary_auto: The Trump administration is scrapping plans to create a $1.8 billion US fund meant to compensate allies of the Republican president, the Justice Department's top official said Tuesday in retreating from a program that faced setbacks in the courts and a fierce political backlash that had threatened to stall key elements of the White House agenda
